snapshot_references_checked.line_16: CEEffectTypes definition includes Snapshot. Remains as dead type. Harmless.
snapshot_references_checked.line_223: CEProduceDirectEffect excludes {MFR, Snapshot}. Still correct: excluding a never-produced type is a no-op.
snapshot_references_checked.line_234_240: CEProcessFill original MFR+Snapshot pair. Target of this change.
snapshot_references_checked.line_334_335: CEProcessRecoveredPending original MFR+Snapshot pair. Target of this change.
snapshot_references_checked.line_774: Next formula excludes {MFR, Snapshot} from CEProduceDirectEffect. Unchanged by this proposal.

downstream_type_agnosticism_verified.CEBackupApply: spec:254-263. Selects by session + seq (next_expected). No type guard. Type-agnostic.
downstream_type_agnosticism_verified.OMSConsumeCE: spec:460-469. Selects by session + seq contiguity (cursor+1). Copies eff.type passively into local_eff. No type-specific branching.
downstream_type_agnosticism_verified.ArchiverIngestCE: spec:627-631. Stores full record unconditionally from msgs_ce_broadcast. No type filter.
downstream_type_agnosticism_verified.ArchiverIngestCEFromBackup: spec:638-645. Selects from ce_log by seq. No type filter.
downstream_type_agnosticism_verified.CEViewChange: spec:298-323. Filters ce_log by e.seq <= recovered. No type guard.

P1_TypeInvariant.trace: ce_log subset of [type: CEEffectTypes, seq: 1..MaxSeq, session: Nat] (spec:122). MFR is in CEEffectTypes (spec:16). All produced effects have type MFR, which is in the domain. Snapshot remains in CEEffectTypes as dead member, no type violation.

P2_CESequenceMonotonic.trace: Uniqueness within session: each fill produces one effect at ce_seq, then increments ce_seq. No two effects in same session share a seq. Identical reasoning to CEProduceDirectEffect which already produces 1 effect per action.

P4_SurgicalRollbackPrecision.trace: Invariant: old-session effects have seq <= ce_last_recovered_seq. Recovery filters by e.seq <= recovered (spec:309). Fewer effects per fill means fewer seqs to recover but contiguity is preserved. MaxContiguousFrom still works: it walks from anchor+1 upward through available archiver seqs.

P8_OMSDerivedState.trace: INV8 (spec:906-909): e.type in CEEffectTypes union OBEffectTypes union {PendingFill}. OMSConsumeCE copies eff.type from CE broadcast (spec:466). MFR effects have type MFR which is in CEEffectTypes. Snapshot no longer produced but Snapshot remaining in CEEffectTypes is irrelevant since INV8 checks membership, not exhaustive production.

P9_CELogBounded.trace: e.seq < ce_seq. Single effect at ce_seq, then ce_seq' = ce_seq+1. Effect has seq = ce_seq (before increment), ce_seq' = ce_seq+1. So e.seq < ce_seq'. Identical to CEProduceDirectEffect pattern.

P12_CEBackupAppliedValid.trace: Every s in ce_backup_applied has a ce_log entry. Backup applies effects from msgs_ce_broadcast which were added to ce_log in the same atomic step. With one effect per fill, backup applies one seq per fill. Each such seq exists in ce_log.

P6_PhantomLeaderRejection.trace: About authority_ts in view change messages. No interaction with effect production.

P16_DownstreamCacheCoherence.trace: About cached timestamps. No interaction with effect production.

P17_OMSMutedUntilCaughtUp.trace: ce_last_recovered_seq computed from archiver contiguity (spec:306). With 1 effect per fill, archiver stores have 1 entry per fill instead of 2. MaxContiguousFrom walks sequentially: if seqs 1,2,3 are available, recovered=3. With fewer total seqs, recovered value is smaller but oms_ce_cursor also advances through fewer seqs. Both sides scale proportionally. Safety condition preserved.

P18_CEArchiverSafetyGuarantee.trace: Three-way disjunction per s in ce_backup_applied (spec:P18 formal def). Each applied seq has: (a) archiver store entry via ArchiverIngestCE, (b) msgs_ce_broadcast entry from the producing action, (c) ce_log entry from the producing action. All three legs hold for single-effect fills identically to how they hold for direct effects.

P19_ArchiverDAMonotonicity.trace: About GC conditions on archiver stores. Effect production count per fill does not affect GC logic (GC is keyed on checkpoint_seq and ack_count). Fewer stored effects per fill reduces GC pressure, pure benefit.

guard_change_verified.assessment: Correct. Matches CEProduceDirectEffect guard (spec:222). Single effect uses ce_seq, increments to ce_seq+1. ce_seq+1 can be MaxSeq+1 (the upper bound sentinel), which is within the ce_seq domain (spec:122 implies ce_seq in 1..(MaxSeq+1)).

unchanged_clauses_verified.CEProcessFill: UNCHANGED clause identical to original. Correct.
unchanged_clauses_verified.CEProcessRecoveredPending: UNCHANGED clause identical to original. Correct.

backup_contiguity_verified.assessment: CEBackupApply expects next_expected = MaxOfSet(ce_backup_applied)+1. Currently fill produces seqs N, N+1 and backup applies both sequentially. After collapse, fill produces only seq N, next action produces N+1. Backup still applies N, N+1 in order. No gap introduced because ce_seq increments by 1 per effect and all effects are broadcast.

observations.description: Snapshot remains in CEEffectTypes (spec:16) as a dead type that is never produced. Proposer correctly notes this as out-of-scope cosmetic follow-up. Agree: removing it would touch the CONSTANTS section and is a separate change.

The CEProduceDirectEffect exclusion set (spec:223) is CEEffectTypes \ {MFR, Snapshot}. After this change, Snapshot is excluded but also never produced by any action. The exclusion is redundant for Snapshot but harmless.

structural_impact_assessment.state_space_reduction: Significant. Each fill-path step produces 1 effect instead of 2, halving the branching factor for fill-heavy workloads. This directly reduces TLC state space.
structural_impact_assessment.seq_space_benefit: Real but bounded by MaxSeq being a model constant. In production reasoning, halving seq consumption for fills extends headroom.
structural_impact_assessment.complexity_reduction: Guard simplification from ce_seq+1<=MaxSeq to ce_seq<=MaxSeq aligns fill actions with direct-effect actions, removing a structural asymmetry.
